text:
There once was a wise old owl named Odgerwiggle. He lived high in an old oak tree, observing the world below. One day, a small mouse approached him with a worried look. The mouse wasn'o just any ordinary mouse—they whispered secrets about the forest's troubles!

Odgerwiggle listened. He knew which leaves tasted of rain and which birds had fallen silent. He knew what storms brewed on the bay.

Finally, he spoke softly. "Tell me true."

The mouse faltered. "The wolf..."

Odgerwiggle nodded. "You're right, he doesn't. He feeds once a moon."

The mouse didn't know what to say.

A shadow loomed. Something big wasn't right. Wolves lived. They had eyes like stars.

Odgerwiggle flew away silently. That night, the howling grew. He came back. Now it was bad enough to scare anyone away.

"You're welcome," he whispered again.
